semantic_description: The function array_add takes length n and three integer arrays a, b, and out. It runs one for_loop from i = 0 to i < n, reads a[i] and b[i], and writes out[i] = a[i] + b[i]. The contract requires n >= 0, all three logical lists have length n, and each elementwise sum fits in signed int range. On return, a and b are unchanged, while out contains a length-n list whose every index equals the corresponding sum. If n is 0, the loop body is skipped and the postcondition is satisfied by the original empty out list.
